vocabulary.definition: a thin layer of ice that forms on cold surfaces
vocabulary.example: Every morning the ground was covered in frost.

vocabulary.definition: a thick, soft fabric often used for warm clothing
vocabulary.example: Hagrid could be seen from the upstairs windows defrosting broomsticks on the Quidditch field, bundled up in a long moleskin overcoat, rabbit fur gloves, and enormous beaverskin boots.

vocabulary.definition: the Quidditch player who catches the Golden Snitch
vocabulary.example: But the news that he was playing Seeker had leaked out somehow, and Harry didn’t know which was worse—people telling him he’d be brilliant or people telling him they’d be running around underneath him holding a mattress.

The day before Harry’s first Quidditch match the three of them were out in the freezing courtyard during break, and she had conjured them up a bright blue fire that could be carried around in a jam jar.

vocabulary.definition: to walk with difficulty because of an injured leg
vocabulary.example: Harry noticed at once that Snape was limping.
One of his legs was bloody and mangled.

vocabulary.definition: something meant to distract people from what is really happening
vocabulary.example: And I’d bet my broomstick he let that troll in, to make a diversion!
Seekers are always the ones who get clobbered by the other team.

vocabulary.definition: to try to persuade someone using a sweet, coaxing voice
vocabulary.example: “Just a bit of toast,” wheedled Hermione.

vocabulary.definition: a device with two lenses used to see faraway things more clearly
to cast a bad-luck spell on someone or something
vocabulary.example: “He’s doing something—jinxing the broom,” said Hermione.

vocabulary.definition: Hagrid's three-headed dog that guards a secret object
vocabulary.example: “How do you know about Fluffy?” he said.

characters.name: Harry Potter
characters.description: Plays his first Quidditch match as Gryffindor Seeker while his broom goes out of control.

characters.name: Hermione Granger
characters.description: Helps with homework, spots Snape jinxing Harry's broom, and sets his robes on fire.

characters.name: Severus Snape
characters.description: Limping and suspicious; Harry believes he is after what the three-headed dog guards.

characters.name: Rubeus Hagrid
characters.description: Watches the match from the stands and accidentally reveals the dog's name and Nicolas Flamel.

background.hook: November brings Harry's first Quidditch match and a shocking glimpse of Snape's injured leg. During the game, Harry's broom spins out of control — and afterward Hagrid lets more than he means to slip.
background.readingFocus: Notice what Harry sees in the staffroom, how Hermione stops Snape during the match, and what Hagrid accidentally says about Fluffy and Nicolas Flamel.

miniCheck.stem: The Seeker's job in Quidditch is to —

options.text: put a harmful spell on it

comprehension.stem: What is this chapter mainly about?

options.text: Harry's first Quidditch match, Snape's suspicious behavior, and new clues about the guarded secret
Harry shopping for school supplies in Diagon Alley
Christmas at Hogwarts and the Invisibility Cloak
Hagrid hatching a dragon egg in his hut

comprehension.explanation: The chapter follows the Gryffindor–Slytherin match, Hermione stopping Snape's jinx, and Hagrid's slip about Fluffy and Nicolas Flamel.

comprehension.stem: What does Harry see when he peeks into the staffroom?

options.text: Snape's bloody, mangled leg while Filch bandages it
Dumbledore reading Quidditch Through the Ages

comprehension.explanation: Snape holds his robes up to show a bloody, mangled leg, and Filch hands him bandages.

comprehension.stem: Why does Snape take five points from Gryffindor in the courtyard?

options.text: Harry has Quidditch Through the Ages outside the school
Ron conjures fire in the corridor
Harry flies his broom before the match

comprehension.explanation: Snape says library books are not to be taken outside the school and confiscates the book.

comprehension.stem: What does Marcus Flint do to Harry during the Snitch chase?

options.text: He catches the Snitch for Slytherin
He swaps Harry's broom for a Comet
He reports Harry to Madam Hooch for cheating

comprehension.explanation: Marcus Flint blocks Harry on purpose, and Harry's broom spins off course.

comprehension.stem: Why does Hermione set fire to the hem of Snape's robes?

options.text: She believes he is jinxing Harry's broom and wants to break his concentration
She wants to warm her hands in the stands
Snape ordered her to practice a fire charm
She is trying to light Hagrid's binoculars

comprehension.explanation: Hermione sees Snape muttering without blinking and jinxing the broom, so she lights his robes.

comprehension.stem: How does Harry catch the Golden Snitch?

options.text: He claps his hand to his mouth and nearly swallows it before it falls into his hand
He traps it in a jam jar Hermione gives him
Fred Weasley hands it to him after beating a Bludger
He grabs it from Marcus Flint's pocket

comprehension.explanation: Harry speeds toward the ground, claps his hand to his mouth, and something gold falls into his hand.

comprehension.stem: What name does Hagrid accidentally reveal in his hut?
comprehension.explanation: Hagrid asks how they know about Fluffy and later mentions Nicolas Flamel before catching himself.

options.text: ice that covers the ground on cold mornings
a spell to light blue fire

vocabulary.explanation: Every morning the ground is covered in frost as November grows colder.

vocabulary.stem: One of his legs was bloody and ___.
vocabulary.explanation: Harry sees Snape's leg bloody and mangled in the staffroom.

vocabulary.stem: Which word is closest in meaning to 'conjure'?
vocabulary.explanation: Hermione conjures a bright blue fire in a jam jar.

vocabulary.stem: "He's doing something—___ the broom," said Hermione.
vocabulary.explanation: Hermione believes Snape is jinxing Harry's broomstick during the match.

options.text: coax someone in a sweet, persuasive way

vocabulary.explanation: Hermione wheedles Harry to eat toast before the match.

cloze.title: Quidditch
paragraphs: November turns icy, and every morning the ground is covered in (1) ___. Harry prepares for his first match as Gryffindor (2) ___ while Hermione helps with homework and lends him Quidditch Through the Ages.
Harry peeks into the staffroom and sees Snape's bloody, (3) ___ leg. In the stands, Ron and Hermione watch through (7) ___ as the match begins.

During the game, Flint fouls Harry and then Harry's broom lurches wildly. Hermione spots Snape (4) ___ the broom and sets fire to his robes. Harry regains control, catches the Snitch, and Gryffindor wins.
In Hagrid's hut afterward, he blurts out the name (5) ___ for the three-headed dog and mentions (6) ___ Flamel before telling them to stop meddling.

gaps.explanation: Cold November mornings leave frost on the ground.
News leaks that Harry is playing Seeker for Gryffindor.
Snape's leg is bloody and mangled when Harry sees him.
Hermione says Snape is jinxing Harry's broom.
Hagrid asks how they know about his dog Fluffy.
Hagrid says the secret is between Dumbledore and Nicolas Flamel.
Many students watch the match through binoculars.
